mission.title: Install and Configure Slimtoolkit on Kubernetes
mission.description: SlimToolkit is a powerful tool that allows developers to optimize, inspect, and debug their container images, making them smaller and more secure without altering the original image. Installing SlimToolkit will help you enhance your container development experience significantly.

steps.title: Create a Namespace
steps.description:
Create a dedicated namespace for SlimToolkit to keep resources organized. Run the following command:
```bash
kubectl create namespace slimtoolkit
```

steps.title: Apply the SlimToolkit Manifests
steps.description:
Download and apply the SlimToolkit manifests directly from the GitHub repository. Use the following commands:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/slimtoolkit/slim/main/deploy/slimtoolkit-deployment.yaml -n slimtoolkit
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/slimtoolkit/slim/main/deploy/slimtoolkit-service.yaml -n slimtoolkit
```

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description:
Check that the SlimToolkit pods are running correctly by executing:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n slimtoolkit
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of SlimToolkit will result in the Slim pods running in the 'slim' namespace, and you will be able to access the service via port 8080 on your local machine. You can also verify that resource limits are set correctly.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/slimtoolkit/slim/main/deploy/slimtoolkit-deployment.yaml -n slimtoolkit
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/slimtoolkit/slim/main/deploy/slimtoolkit-service.yaml -n slimtoolkit

uninstall.title: Remove SlimToolkit Resources
uninstall.description:
To uninstall SlimToolkit, delete the resources you created. Run the following commands:
```bash
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/slimtoolkit/slim/main/deploy/slimtoolkit-deployment.yaml -n slimtoolkit
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/slimtoolkit/slim/main/deploy/slimtoolkit-service.yaml -n slimtoolkit
kubectl delete namespace slimtoolkit
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade SlimToolkit
upgrade.description:
To upgrade SlimToolkit, first delete the existing resources and then reapply the updated manifests. Use the following commands:
```bash
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/slimtoolkit/slim/main/deploy/slimtoolkit-deployment.yaml -n slimtoolkit
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/slimtoolkit/slim/main/deploy/slimtoolkit-service.yaml -n slimtoolkit
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/slimtoolkit/slim/main/deploy/slimtoolkit-deployment.yaml -n slimtoolkit
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/slimtoolkit/slim/main/deploy/slimtoolkit-service.yaml -n slimtoolkit
```

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the SlimToolkit pod is not starting, check the pod logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n slimtoolkit
```

troubleshooting.title: Service Not Accessible
troubleshooting.description:
If the service is not accessible, verify the service configuration and ensure the correct ports are exposed:
```bash
kubectl describe service slimtoolkit -n slimtoolkit
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter resource allocation issues, ensure your Kubernetes cluster has enough resources available. You can check node resources with:
```bash
kubectl describe nodes
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to interact with your cluster.
